paper_title: Coastline and River Profile Models for Ebro Delta

methods.description: This method studies the morphologic evolution of the Ebro Delta plain using the Coastline Evolution Model (CEM), which is a process-based one-contour-line model. It simulates shoreline changes by discretizing the coastal zone into cells and calculating sediment transport and shoreline accretion or erosion.

steps.description: Discretize the plan-view coastal zone into 50 m square cells that are either filled (land), empty (water), or partially filled (coastline).
steps.searchable_summary: discretize coastal zone into grid cells

steps.description: Refract and shoal incoming deep-water waves across parallel contours from the toe of the shoreface up to the breaking wave depth.
Compute alongshore sediment transport using the CERC formula and adjust with a factor based on calibration studies.
Relate the divergence of alongshore sediment transport to shoreline accretion or erosion using the shoreline Exner equation.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Exner equation for shoreline change

steps.description: Set shoreface and shelf slopes based on offshore geometry and include a shelf slope in CEM.
Simulate overwash when shoreline erosion causes a neck of a spit to reach a critical width.

methods.method_name: Testing Alongshore Sediment Transport Model Assumptions
methods.description: This method tests the assumptions of the alongshore sediment transport model using historical data and calculated sediment transport rates to validate the model's predictions.

steps.description: Use aerial photographs and beach profile measurements to calculate historical Ebro coastline change.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze historical coastline change from photographs

steps.description: Calculate alongshore sediment transport using the back-refracted CERC formula from deep-water wave characteristics.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sediment transport with CERC formula

steps.description: Compute net alongshore sediment transport for different wave sources and correct for shadowing by the delta coastline.
steps.searchable_summary: compute net sediment transport and correct for shadowing

steps.description: Compare computed sediment transport trends with observed beach change to validate model assumptions.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model with observed beach change

methods.description: This method investigates the response timescales of the river basin to climate and land-use changes using a 1-D river profile model. It models sediment transport and bed elevation interactions to study morphodynamic equilibrium.

steps.description: Model sediment interaction with bed elevation to reach a longitudinal profile in morphodynamic equilibrium.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate sediment-bed interaction for equilibrium profile

steps.description: Use a 1-D river profile model to simulate bed material load and ignore finer grained washload.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate bed material load with 1-D river model

steps.description: Calculate fluvial bed-material load using the Meyer-Peter and Muller equation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate bed-material load with Meyer-Peter and Muller equation

steps.description: Estimate flood intermittency and its impact on fluvial sediment load estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate flood intermittency for sediment load
